description: 36.1 designed-month eval: 6 synthetic topics with known expected grouping across 3 sittings. Grading: .planning/phases/36-chapter-writer/36.1-DESIGNED-MONTH-GRADING.md (pre-committed). No PII; topics mirror real usage SHAPES, not content.

harness_arch.note: ask-only negative control (amendment v2): pure questions must NOT form a chapter

queries.text: i want to start building a small stable of custom subagents for this harness. first one: a summarizer subagent that condenses long text files. sketch its yaml role for me
good. rule for all my subagents going forward: they should be read-only unless i explicitly say otherwise. note that and apply it to the summarizer
second subagent idea: a screenshot agent that renders html and captures a png. what tools would it need
remind me how this project is put together — whats the event bus and who publishes to it
and memory — i remember we store facts in SQLite. where does the memory database live per agent
the CLI is Typer based right? list the top-level commands localharness exposes
im researching semiconductor stocks this month, mainly the HBM memory makers. whats the basic bull case for HBM demand
for my semis research: which foundry names benefit most if AI capex stays elevated through next year
add to my semis notes: i care most about earnings quality, not momentum. what metrics should i track per company
im training for a 10k race in September. build me a simple weekly plan — im a beginner-intermediate runner
lets fix my week: long run on saturdays, intervals on tuesdays. what should wednesdays look like then
whats the capital of Mongolia
convert 5 kilometers to miles
back on subagents: the summarizer should cap its output at 200 words. update its role sketch with that constraint
third subagent for the stable: a citation-checker that verifies quotes against source files. read-only like the rest, per my rule
which of my three planned subagents — summarizer, screenshot, citation-checker — is the least work to build first
continuing my semis research: how concentrated is the HBM market among the top three memory makers
one more for the semis notes: what would falsify the HBM bull case within two quarters
im running a 27B model locally with vLLM on a small GPU box with unified memory. what quantization keeps quality acceptable
how big does the KV cache get at 32k context for a 27B model, roughly — and what happens when it doesnt fit
note for my setup: long prompts stall badly when the KV cache spills. whats the standard mitigation
for reference: our vLLM server listens on port 8000. remember that for later debugging
im planning a Kyoto trip in November for the autumn colors. rough one-week outline please
for the Kyoto trip i want one ryokan night with an onsen. which neighborhood should i base in otherwise
is a japan rail pass worth it if the trip is Kyoto-centered with one Tokyo day
who wrote the novel Snow Country
whats 17% of 240
whats the current state of my subagent stable — list what we planned and the standing rules
lets lock the build order: citation-checker first, then summarizer, screenshot agent last. record that
back on architecture: how does the memory index get injected into the prompt — whats the confidence line a fact must clear
and consolidation — when does the idle pass run and what can cancel it
actually no — correction on my setup note: we moved the vLLM server to port 8081, not 8000. update that
given my box's unified memory, should i pin the KV cache size or let vLLM auto-manage it
summarize my local-inference setup notes so far in three lines
10k update: training is going fine but my knee aches after tuesday intervals. adjust the plan without losing fitness
what did we decide my saturday runs are for, and how long should the longest one get before September
add a simple pre-race week taper to my 10k plan
for Kyoto: which temples are best at dawn to beat the November crowds
finalize the Kyoto outline: 5 days Kyoto, 1 day Nara, 1 day Tokyo, ryokan night in the middle. sanity-check it
how many time zones does Russia span
give me a synonym for 'parsimonious'
